anchor.snippet: the common logarithm (aka "standard logarithm") is the logarithm with base 10
The fractional part is known as the mantissa
The integer part, called the characteristic , can be computed by simply counting how many places the decimal point must be moved

annotations.label: Negative logarithms of numbers less than 1

anchor.snippet: Positive numbers less than 1 have negative logarithms
a special notation, called bar notation, is used
The following example uses the bar notation to calculate 0.012 × 0.85 = 0.0102
